questions.stem: Figure 1 shows an armature-controlled DC motor. Find the transfer function θ(s)/Ea(s) for this motor.
common_errors: Forgetting to include back-EMF term Kb*ω in armature equation
Not accounting for mechanical load inertia J and friction f in torque equation
Incorrect LaPlace transform of d/dt terms
questions.solution_method: Write KVL for armature circuit: Ea = ia*Ra + La*(dia/dt) + Eb. Write torque-speed equation: Tm = J*(dω/dt) + f*ω where Tm = Kt*ia and Eb = Kb*ω. Take Laplace transforms, eliminate Ia(s) and Ω(s) to get θ(s)/Ea(s) = Kt / [s*(La*s + Ra)*(J*s + f) + Kt*Kb*s].

questions.stem: The motor transfer function G(s) = Km/(τ′s+1) controls speed ωo(s) in closed-loop with tachometer feedback Kt and forward gain K (Fig. 2). Determine the value of K such that the closed-loop time constant τ″ = 0.1τ′. Given: τ′ = 0.1, Km = 1.25, Kt = 0.2.
common_errors: Incorrectly forming the closed-loop TF — missing the Kt block in feedback path
questions.solution_method: Form T(s) = ωo(s)/Vr(s). The closed-loop TF with unity forward path K and tachometer feedback Kt is T(s) = K*Km / (τ′s + 1 + K*Km*Kt). Identify closed-loop time constant τ″ = τ′/(1 + K*Km*Kt). Set τ″ = 0.1τ′, solve: 1 + K*Km*Kt = 10, giving K = 9/(Km*Kt) = 9/(1.25*0.2) = 36.

questions.stem: For the same tachometer-feedback speed control system, find the expression for sensitivity S_Kt^T and its value at ω = 0 rad/sec.
common_errors: Confusing numerator and denominator partial derivatives
questions.solution_method: Use S_Kt^T = (Kt/T)*(∂T/∂Kt). With T(s) = K*Km/(τ′s + 1 + K*Km*Kt), compute ∂T/∂Kt = -K²*Km²/(τ′s+1+K*Km*Kt)². Thus S_Kt^T = -K*Km*Kt/(τ′s+1+K*Km*Kt). At ω=0 (s=0): S_Kt^T = -K*Km*Kt/(1+K*Km*Kt). Substitute known values to get the numerical answer.

questions.stem: Find the transfer function VL(s)/V(s) for the electrical network in Fig. 3, which contains a nonlinear resistor with voltage-current relationship ir = 2*e^(0.1*Vr), where ir and Vr are the resistor current and voltage. The circuit has a 1H inductor in series with the nonlinear resistor, with a 20V DC bias source.
common_errors: Forgetting to find the operating point (DC bias) before linearising
Incorrect linearisation: slope of ir vs Vr must be evaluated at the operating point
Not replacing the nonlinear resistor with its linearised equivalent conductance in the small-signal circuit
Sign errors in KVL with the 20V source
questions.solution_method: Step 1 — Find operating point: with v(t)=0, inductor is short in DC steady state, so Vr = 20V and ir0 = 2*e^(0.1*20) = 2*e^2. Step 2 — Linearise: small-signal conductance g = dir/dVr|_Q = 2*0.1*e^(0.1*Vr0) = 0.2*e^2. Equivalent small-signal resistance r = 1/g. Step 3 — Small-signal circuit: v(s) drives L=1H in series with r (linearised resistor); VL(s) = voltage across inductor = V(s)*[Ls/(Ls+r)] = V(s)*[s/(s + r)].

notes: Tutorial dated 3 February 2026. 3 questions (Q2 split into 2a/2b), 4 entries total. Figures referenced are circuit/block diagrams embedded in the PDF. Q1 and Q3 require figure interpretation; stems faithfully paraphrased from visible text.
